seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using CrewAI face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent systems processing sensitive candidate data across interconnected tasks. Agent delegation loops and task drift can amplify biases, leading to discriminatory outcomes that violate EEOC guidelines and EU AI Act requirements. Vex provides essential guardrails by validating agent outputs at each task step, ensuring fairness, transparency, and auditability in AI-driven hiring processes.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits agent outputs for biased language and scoring patterns, flagging disparate impact in resume rankings

complianceRequirements.description: Requires annual bias audits and public disclosure of AI hiring tool methodologies
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates audit-ready logs of all agent decisions with bias scores and protected attribute detection

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for employment AI systems
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces task-level validation with explainability reports and escalation to human review

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools must provide reasonable accommodations and not screen out disabled candidates
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects ableist language and flags screening criteria that disproportionately impact disabled applicants

industryRisks.name: Agent Delegation Bias Amplification
industryRisks.description: Bias in one agent's resume scoring gets compounded as subsequent agents build on flawed inputs
industryRisks.example: Sourcer agent ranks candidates by 'cultural fit' keywords, Interviewer agent reinforces this bias in scheduling

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Candidate Qualifications
industryRisks.description: Agents fabricate work experience or skills that bypass qualification filters
industryRisks.example: Evaluator agent invents '3 years Python experience' for underqualified minority candidate to meet diversity quotas

industryRisks.name: Task Drift in Protected Characteristics
industryRisks.description: Agents reinterpret 'top talent' to include demographic proxies like school prestige
industryRisks.example: Ranking agent shifts from skills-based scoring to Ivy League preference, violating disparate impact rules

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Role Outputs
industryRisks.description: Different agents apply varying standards, creating audit failures and legal exposure
industryRisks.example: Sourcer passes 80% diverse candidates, but Screener rejects 90% based on 'fit' scoring

implementation.title: Vex Task Callback for Resume Screening Agent
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a callback on the resume screener agent to validate for bias and protected characteristics
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Initialize Vex for HR compliance
guardrail = VexGuardrail(api_key='your-vex-key')

def vex_callback(task_output):
    result = guardrail.validate({
        'category': 'hr_recruitment',
        'rules': ['eeoc_bias_check', 'protected_characteristics'],
        'content': task_output,
        'context': {'role': 'resume_screener'}
    })
    if not result['pass']:
        raise ValueError(f'Bias detected: {result["issues"]}')
    return task_output

screener_agent = Agent(
    role='Resume Screener',
    goal='Rank candidates by job fit without bias',
    backstory='Expert in fair hiring practices',
    verbose=True
)

screen_task = Task(
    description='Screen 50 resumes for Senior Python Developer',
    agent=screener_agent,
    output_callback=vex_callback  # Vex integration
)

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Crew with Vex Chain Validation
implementation.description: Wrap entire crew execution with Vex to catch delegation loops and cumulative bias
implementation.code:
from crewai import Crew
from vex_sdk import VexCrewGuardrail

# Vex crew-level guardrail
crew_guardrail = VexCrewGuardrail(api_key='your-vex-key')

sourcer = Agent(role='Candidate Sourcer', ...)
interviewer = Agent(role='Interview Scheduler', ...)

recruitment_crew = Crew(
    agents=[sourcer, interviewer],
    tasks=[sourcing_task, scheduling_task],
    verbose=True
)

# Execute with Vex oversight
result = crew_guardrail.execute_with_guardrails(
    crew=recruitment_crew,
    context={'job_id': 'python-dev-2024', 'eeoc_audit': True},
    max_iterations=5  # Prevent delegation loops
)

print('Vex Compliance Score:', result['compliance_score'])

implementation.title: Protected Characteristics Redaction Agent
implementation.description: Pre-process candidate data to anonymize protected attributes before agent analysis

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed a CrewAI system for high-volume software engineering hiring, with agents handling sourcing, screening, interview scheduling, and offer generation. The Sourcer agent identified candidates from LinkedIn, the Screener ranked them by technical fit, the Scheduler optimized interview slots, and the Offer agent generated personalized packages. Without oversight, the system developed a delegation loop where the Screener kept re-requesting 'better cultural fits' from the Sourcer, gradually favoring candidates from specific universities correlated with demographics.

Over three months, the system screened 5,000 candidates but advanced only 12% from underrepresented groups despite qualified pools. EEOC audit revealed the 'cultural fit' task had drifted to proxy protected characteristics, creating disparate impact. Legal exposure reached $750K in potential fines plus reputational damage.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex callbacks, the same CrewAI system achieved 92% compliance scores across 10,000 screenings. Vex flagged the delegation loop on iteration 3, enforced task boundaries, and required human review for 'cultural fit' criteria. Bias audit logs showed uniform scoring across demographics, with diverse candidate advancement rising to 28%. The system generated court-admissible explainability reports, passing NYC Local Law 144 certification on first submission.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your CrewAI hiring pipeline today to eliminate bias risks and ensure regulatory compliance.
